creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
What do you think of that last burger, eh? 😅

London has a reputation for being a pretty expensive travel destination - here are a few tips on how to get more bang for your buck in the English capital: 

🏛️ A lot of the museums in London are free, BUT you sometimes need to pre-book a time slot so make sure you check out their website before going 

🚇 Make sure you use the same contactless card (or get an Oyster Card) when you use public transport - there’s a daily cap so there’s a limit to how much you’re going to pay a day 🤓

🚶BUT consider walking instead of using public transport - London is massive so you WILL be using public transport, but a lot of the charm of London is IMO the small side streets and the beautiful buildings (and you’re not going to see them on the Underground) 

🏙️ As soon as you know when you’re going to be in London, book free tickets to a viewing platform like Skygarden or Horizon 22. The view from up there is incredible and it’s free, but the tickets “sell out” very fast 🤓

Share your own London travel hacks in a comment 🥰
 #london #londontravel #travelvlog

transcription.text: Here's how much I spent having a rather lovely day in London. I'd completely forgotten how people actually like Coldplay and voted for the Nazis, so I trusted the online reviews of this brunch place in Covent Garden, but you just can't trust people, guys. This hotel's location is a 10 out of 10, but what's happened to pint prices in London? You used to be able to get cheap drinks at the Sam Smith's pubs, but nowadays it's just as expensive as anywhere else. In school I was a bit of an outsider when it came to music, and although I never made the leap to wearing eyeliner, I felt right at home when I came to Camden for the first time as a 14 year old. Back then I didn't care about museums, if there wasn't a guitar played by Pete Doherty in it, I think my mum would say that nothing's changed there, but look at me having a bloody good time at the British Museum, mum. A food court in an old church is such a good idea, but the bloke who tricks people into buying this cheeseburger for 17 quid is the true mastermind here.
